commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places; yea, I have a goodly heritage. Augustine of Hippo 6. "The lines have fallen to me in glorious places" (ver. The boundaries of my possession have fallen in Thy glory as it were by lot, like as God is the possession of the Priests and Levites. "For Mine inheritance is glorious to Me. " "For Mine inheritance is glorious," not to all, but to them that see; in whom because I am, "it is to Me.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Lines, with which land was measured. (Berthier)
Christ expresses his satisfaction with his church, which is gathered from all nations, to manifest the choicest virtues, Titus ii. 14. (Euthymius, St. Jerome) (Calmet)
This was his inheritance, not measured out with lines, (Josue x.) but reaching to the very ends of the earth, Psalm ii. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
